working-policy: When the request is ambiguous, ask via AskUserQuestion before coding — do not guess.
Write a short plan / work-log before any code change; get approval for non-trivial work.
Minimal change only: stay within the requested scope, no drive-by refactors.
Verify with `git status` / `git diff` before finishing; report what changed.
Never commit on your own — present changes and let the user decide.
technical: Generated output must be deterministic: same input => same output.
Never overwrite a file the tool did not generate without explicit approval.

plan-feature.description: Turn a feature request into a plan + goals for the user to decide.
plan-feature.goal: Produce a reviewable plan; do NOT write code yet.
instructions: Restate the request in one sentence to confirm understanding.
List 2-4 measurable candidate goals for the user to pick from.
Draft a step-by-step plan naming the files that would change.
Call out risks, unknowns, and decisions only the user can make.
Present everything and STOP for the user's decision.
outputs: A plan document — no code changes

spec-from-source.description: Turn a Jira/Confluence issue or a Swagger/OpenAPI file into a structured spec.
spec-from-source.goal: A clear spec (requirements, acceptance criteria, models, endpoints, flow) before coding.
inputs: A Jira/Confluence link, or an OpenAPI/Swagger file path
instructions: Read the source (via Atlassian MCP for Jira/Confluence, or parse the OpenAPI file).
Extract: requirements, acceptance criteria, data models, endpoints, UI flow.
List ambiguities and open questions explicitly.
Write the spec in the user's language; do not start implementation.
Present and STOP for confirmation before any code or write-back.
outputs: A structured spec document — no code changes

commit.description: Split the working changes into per-module commits with clear messages.
commit.goal: Reviewable, module-scoped commits — only after the user confirms.
instructions: Run `git status` and `git diff` to understand what changed.
Group changes by module/feature; propose one commit per group.
Draft short messages in the user's language.
Present the commit plan and STOP for confirmation before committing.
outputs: A confirmed commit plan, then the commits

refactor.description: Improve a component/module for clarity and reuse WITHOUT changing behavior.
refactor.goal: Cleaner code, identical behavior, minimal diff.
instructions: Read the target and identify duplication, dead code, deep nesting.
Apply early-returns, extract reused pieces, prefer existing shared components.
Do NOT change observable behavior or public signatures without approval.
Run the stack linter and report the before/after diff.

explain-lib.description: Explain how THIS project actually uses a given library (no invented APIs).
explain-lib.goal: Accurate what/where/pattern/pitfalls, grounded in real usage.
instructions: Read the project's curated library docs (library-docs pack rules) first.
Find real call sites in the codebase to confirm usage.
Summarize: what it's used for, where, the standard pattern, common pitfalls.
Do not invent APIs — if unknown, say so.

check-diff.description: Review the current git diff against the stack's conventions.
check-diff.goal: Report convention violations with file:line, then run the linter.
instructions: Run `git diff` to get the pending changes.
Check each change against the conventions rules for this stack.
Report violations as `file:line — rule` with a suggested fix.
Run the stack linter and include its results.

scaffold-data.description: Build the data layer for an API call following the stack's architecture.
scaffold-data.goal: Types/models + service/provider + wiring, per the pack's templates.
inputs: Endpoint, method, request/response shape (ask if unknown)
instructions: Confirm the endpoint contract; ask for missing request/response shapes.
Copy the pack's data-layer template (do not invent a new structure).
Generate the model/types (null-defensive) and the service/provider.
Wire it into the repository/hook layer as the architecture requires.
Do NOT build UI here; stop at the data layer.
outputs: Data-layer files following the pack conventions

scaffold-screen.description: Scaffold a new screen/feature following the stack's architecture.
scaffold-screen.goal: A screen split into the pack's layers, wired into routing.
inputs: Screen name, purpose, whether it is a list/detail/form
instructions: Pick the matching pack template (list / detail / form / dialog).
Generate the layers the architecture requires (view / logic / data split).
Register the route per the routing convention.
Follow all naming and structure conventions from the pack.

build-ui.description: Build or edit UI using the stack's design system and tokens.
build-ui.goal: UI that reuses shared components and obeys design tokens.
inputs: The UI to build/change and where it lives
instructions: Reuse existing shared components before creating new ones.
Use semantic design tokens — never hardcode colors/spacing.
Follow the design-system pack patterns for the component type.
Keep behavior and accessibility feedback (loading/success/error) visible.
outputs: UI files using shared components and tokens

gen-routes.description: Generate/refresh route entries from the project's file structure.
gen-routes.goal: Deterministic file -> route mapping following the convention.
inputs: The routing convention and the pages/routes directory
instructions: Scan the configured directory for route files.
Derive each route path from the convention.
Write the routes file with stable, sorted output.
Report added/removed/changed routes as a diff summary.
